---
title: "aslbench Run Report"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    format-links: false
  typst:
    toc: true
params:
  run_dir: ""
  output_fmt: "html"
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
# | tags: [parameters]
# | echo: false
run_dir = ""
output_fmt = "html"
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import HTML, Image, Markdown, display

from aslbench import config, figures, runner, scoring

run_dir = Path(run_dir)
run_slug = run_dir.name

config_data = json.loads((run_dir / "config.json").read_text())
results = runner.model_results_list(run_slug)
nonempty = [r for r in results if not r.df.empty]
labels = [r.model_label for r in nonempty]
colors = figures.assign_colors(labels)

_OUTPUT_FMT = output_fmt  # "html" for HTML output, "typst" for PDF


def _show_fig(fig):
    """Render a Plotly figure interactively (HTML) or as a PNG (Typst/PDF)."""
    if _OUTPUT_FMT == "html":
        fig.show()
    else:
        import plotly.io as pio
        img_bytes = pio.to_image(fig, format="png", width=900, height=500)
        display(Image(img_bytes))


def _fmt_table(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Format float columns, replacing NaN with '--'."""
    out = df.copy()
    for col in out.select_dtypes(include="float").columns:
        out[col] = out[col].apply(lambda v: f"{v:.3f}" if pd.notna(v) else "--")
    return out
```

## Run metadata

```{python}
# | echo: false
lines = [
    f"- **Run:** {config_data['run_slug']}",
    f"- **Started:** {config_data['started_at']}",
    f"- **Images per class:** {config_data.get('n_per_class')}",
    f"- **Classes:** {config_data.get('n_classes')}",
    f"- **Total images:** {config_data['n_items']}",
    f"- **Sample seed:** {config_data['sample_seed']}",
    f"- **Prompt template:** {config_data['template_id']}",
    f"- **Note:** {config_data.get('run_note') or '(none)'}",
]
Markdown("\n".join(lines))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
model_rows = [
    {"Provider": m["provider_label"], "Model": m["model_label"], "Slug": m["model_slug"]}
    for m in config_data["models"]
]
display(HTML(pd.DataFrame(model_rows).to_html(index=False)))
```

The task is 36-way single-character classification (ASL fingerspelling digits
0-9 and letters A-Z). Every model saw the identical sampled images, so all
differences are attributable to the model rather than the sample. Filenames,
which encode the true class, are never shown to the models.

## Comparison

```{python}
# | echo: false
if nonempty:
    display(HTML(_fmt_table(scoring.comparison_table(nonempty)).to_html(index=False)))
    display(Markdown(
        "- **Accuracy** — the share of images the model labelled correctly, from "
        "0 to 1; simply how often it is right.\n"
        "- **Macro F1** — the model's reliability averaged across all 36 "
        "characters, giving each character equal weight and blending how often "
        "its guesses are right (precision) with how often it finds each "
        "character (recall). It rewards models that do well on every character, "
        "not just the easy ones.\n"
        "- **MCC** — the Matthews correlation coefficient: a single overall "
        "quality score from -1 to +1 that accounts for every kind of mistake at "
        "once. 0 means no better than random guessing and 1 is perfect.\n"
        "- **Parse failure rate** — how often the model's reply could not be read "
        "as a valid answer (it did not clearly name one of the 36 characters).\n"
        "- **Provider error rate** — how often the model's API failed to return "
        "any response at all (timeouts or errors).\n"
        "- **Chance (1/36)** — a reference column showing what each score would be "
        "for a model that guesses at random."
    ))
else:
    display(Markdown("No results recorded."))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
if nonempty:
    _show_fig(figures.accuracy_bar(nonempty, colors))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
diff_fig = figures.per_class_diff_bars(nonempty, colors) if len(nonempty) >= 2 else None
if diff_fig is not None:
    _show_fig(diff_fig)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
if nonempty:
    _show_fig(figures.per_class_accuracy_bars(nonempty, colors))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
if nonempty:
    _show_fig(figures.confusion_heatmaps(nonempty, colors))
    display(Markdown(
        "**How to read this:** each row is the true character and each column is "
        "what the model guessed. Cells are row-normalized, so a value is the "
        "fraction of that character's images that received the correct guess; the diagonal is "
        "the model's recall for each character. A bright diagonal means accurate "
        "recognition, while bright off-diagonal cells show characters the model "
        "routinely mixes up (e.g. reading 'O' as '0')."
    ))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
if len(nonempty) >= 2:
    mt = scoring.mcnemar_table(nonempty)
    if not mt.empty:
        display(Markdown("### McNemar test"))
        display(HTML(_fmt_table(mt).to_html(index=False)))
        display(Markdown(
            "**What this answers:** whether two models have genuinely different "
            "accuracy, judged only on the images where they disagree. Because "
            "every model saw the exact same images, we compare them "
            "image-by-image. The test looks only at *discordant items*, that is, images "
            "where one model was right and the other was wrong. Images they both "
            "got right or both got wrong say nothing about which is better, so "
            "they are set aside. If those disagreements are far more lopsided "
            "toward one model than a coin flip would explain, the difference is "
            "judged real. In the table, **only_a_correct** and **only_b_correct** "
            "count the discordant images each model won, **better** names the "
            "model ahead on them, and a **p_value** below 0.05 means the gap is "
            "statistically significant (unlikely to be luck)."
        ))
```

## Per-model detail

```{python}
# | echo: false
for res in nonempty:
    display(Markdown(f"### {res.model_label}"))

    mc = scoring.most_confused(res.df, top_n=8)
    wrong = res.df[(res.df["parse_ok"]) & (~res.df["correct"])].head(8)
    wrong_cols = wrong[["item_id", "true_char", "predicted_char"]].reset_index(drop=True)

    cells = []
    if not mc.empty:
        cells.append(
            f'<td valign="top" style="padding-right:2rem">'
            f'<strong>Most-confused pairs</strong><br>'
            f'{mc.to_html(index=False)}</td>'
        )
    if not wrong.empty:
        cells.append(
            f'<td valign="top">'
            f'<strong>Sample misclassifications</strong><br>'
            f'{wrong_cols.to_html(index=False)}</td>'
        )
    if cells:
        display(HTML(
            f'<table style="border:none">'
            f'<tr style="border:none">{" ".join(cells)}</tr>'
            f'</table>'
        ))
```
